content.full_text:
Fern Porrrrcar- PRACrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
October 23,2006
LauraA. Taylor
Planning Commissioner
City of Citrus Heights
6530 Grey Oak Court
Citrus Heights, CA 95621
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No.I-06-189
I Goyemment Code sections g1000 - 91014
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Commission regulations appear attitle 2, sections
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 I I 4; iegulation ttzis 1c1,copy enclosed)
Dear Ms. Taylor:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r Ft"ur""oot" thJ l""u.rr" *"
do not have information sufficient to fully answer your questions, we provide you with
informal assistance.2 The Commission will not advise *ith...p""t to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(bX8XA).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to
evaluate any conduct thatmay have already taken place,and any conclusions contained
herein apply only to prospective actions.
QUESTTON
Do you, as a member of the planning commission of the City of Citrus Heights,
have a conflict of interest under the Act in voting on issues relatingio mobile home parks
based on your father residing in a mobile home park and being pre-sident of the park,s
homeowners' association?
CONCLUSION
Because the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to o,financial
interests" held bypublic officials or membersof their immediate iamily,il ;;* father


FileNo. I-06-189
Page No. 2
is not considered a member of your "immediate family" forpurposes of section g7103 of
the Act, you would not have a conflict of interest in dicisions irn olving mobile home
parks, provided, however, that such decisions would have no effect ,pJ, yo* personal
finances as set forth herein.
You are currently a member of the planning commission for the City of Cikus
Heights (the "Planning Commission"). You are also a candidate in the eleciion for a seat
on the city council.' Your father is a resident of a mobile home community and president
of their homeowner's association. In our telephone conversation of October ll,2006,
you advised that you do not reside with your father, your father is not a source of income
to you, and that you do not have a financial interest in the real property on which his
mobile home is located. You did, however, mention that your fathermakes Christmas
gifts to your children. You will be called upon as a planning commissioner to vote on
issues relating to mobile home parks.
FACTS
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will .,perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused Uy trreir own finuncial interests
or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported them.,, (Section gl00l(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits anypublic official from-making, participating in making,'or
otherwise using his or her official position to influencr u gorr*ental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
The Commission has adopted an_eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifring conflict of interest. (Regulation f aZOOlU;.; the generat
rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public om"iat makes a
governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests.
Step 1. Are you a 66public official" within the meaning of section g7100?
section 82048 defines a public official as ,.every member, officer, employee or
consultant of a state or local government agency." As a member of the fianning
3 Inasmuch as yol have not yet been elected to the city council, your question about conflicts of
interest, if elected, is hypothetical. The Commission may decline to provide even ,,infonnal assistance,, if
the question presented is pwely hlpothetical or-if the facts presented are insufficr""t o. too rugre to render
specific informal assistance. (Regulation 18329.) Accordingly, we limit our advice Jyo* q;.rtio,
involving yourposition as a planning commissioner.


File No. I-06-189
Page No. 3
Commission, which is a local govefirment agency, you are a public official.a Therefore,
you may not make, participate in making, or otherwise use your position to influenc e any
decisions that will have areasonably foreseeable matenal financial effect on any of youi
economic interests.
Step 2. wiII you be making, participating in making or influencing a
governmental decision?
A public official "makes a goveflrmental decision" when the official , acting
within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contrictual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Regulati on 18702.L) A public official
"participates in a governmental decision" when, actrngwithin the auihority of his or her
position and without significant substantive or intervening review, the offitial negotiates,
advises, or makes recommendations to the decisionmakerregarding the governmental
decision. (Regulation 18702.2.) A public official is attempting to ise h[ or her official
position to influence a goveflrmental decision if for the purpose of influencing the
decision, the official contacts or appears before or otherwise attempts to influenco, ffiy
member, officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Regulation 1S7OZ.3.)
Your question presupposes your making or participatingin making decisions regarding
mobile home parks in your capacity as a planning commissioner.
Step 3. What are your economic interests?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic interests. These economic interests are described in
section 87103 and regulations 18703 -lB703.5,inclusive:
o d public official has an economic interest in abusiness entity in which he
or she has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more (section Sliol(a);regulation
18703.1(a), or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, "-pioy"", o,
holds anyposition of management. (Section s7103(d); regulation ttiol.i.1s"
When a public official who holds an office specified in section 87200 hasa conflict of interest in
a decision noticed at a public meeting, then he or she must (1) immediately prior to the discussion of the
item, orally identify each tlpe of economic interest involved in the decision as well as details of the
economic interest, as discussed in regulation 18702.5(b), on the record of the meeting; (z) recuse himself or
herself; and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion andlor vote on the item. For closed
sessions, consent calendars, absences and speaking as a member ofthe public regarding personal interests,
special rules found in regulation 18702.5(c) and 18702.5(d) apply.
- - 
s 
.For purposes of section 87103, "indirect investrnent or interest means any investrnent or interest
owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official..." (Section Bzll3,fu.t p-ug.uphJ


File No. I-06-189
Page No. 4
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she
has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section S7f 0:O); regulation
t8703.2)6
o An official has an economic interest in any solrce of income, including
promised income, totaling $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
871 03(c); regulation 1 8703.3.)
o d public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her
if the gifts total $360 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. ("Section 87103(e);
regulation 18703.4.)
o { public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediate family. This is
commonly referred to as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section S7iO3; regulation
18703.5.)
According to your facts, the mobile home belongs to your father, and he leases
space from the mobile home park owner. We have previously advise a itatwhile a
mobile home itself is an asset, it does not constitute-arealproperty interest because it is
considered to be personal property. (Jorgenson Adviceletter, No. a-qo-ol7). His
interest in the space he rents, however, may be an interest in real property if he has the
right to occupy the space for a period longer than one month and iheleasehold interest is
worth $2,000 or more. (Section 82}33;regulation 18233; TraylorAdvice Letter,
No. 4-95-393; Woodworth Advice Letter, No. A-91-234.)
"Immediate family" is defined at section 82029 as an official,s spouse and
dependent children.
Given the circumstances that (1) you have no economic interest in any leasehold
interest your father mayhave, (2) your father is not a source of income to you, and (3) he
is not a member of your immediate family, you are not prohibited from participating in
any govemmental decision concerning mobile home parks unless such decisions will
have an effect upon your personal finances.
A public official always has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediate family. you have
indicated that your father makes gifts to your children, who are membe6 of yo*
immediate family. Any gifts he makes to your spouse or your dependent children would
be considered under the "personal effects" rule if the valul of these gifts increases or
decreases as a result of any governmental decision you make or participate in making.
(Section 87 103; regulation 1 8703.5.)
6 See note 5


File No. I-06-189
Page No. 5
Step 4. WiII your economic interest be directly or indirectly involved in
decisions you will make or participate in making as a planning commissioner?
Regulation 18704.5 provides that"apublic official or his or her immediate family
are deemed to be directly involved in a goveflrmental decision which has any financial
effect on his or her personal finances or those of his or her immediate family.,, Hence, if
it is reasonably foreseeable that a govemmental decision will have any financial effect on
your personal finances, you are deemed to be directly involved in the decision.
Step 5. What is the applicable materialify standard?
'A reasonably foreseeable financial effect on a public official's personal finances
is material if it is at least $250 in any l2-month period.;' lR"gulation ttizOs.s(a).)
Step 6. Is it reasonably foreseeable that the financial effect of a
governmental decision on your economic interest wiII meet the applicable
materiality standard?
Under regulation 18706, an effect upon economic interests is considered
reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. you have not
advised us of any circumstances suggestingthatyou will be making decisions that may
affect your personal finances to a material degree. If, however, it is reasonably
foreseeable that a govemmental decision will result in the value of the gifts your father
gives to you or members of your immediate family by $250 or more *ithin any 12-month
period, you will have a conflict of interest with respect to that decision. UltimatelS
whether amateial financial effect is foreseeable aithe time a decision is made depends
on facts and circumstances peculiar to each case. (In re Thorner, (1975) I FppC Ops.
198.) The foreseeability of a particular financial effect is a determination that must be
left, in most instances, to the informed judgment of the public official.
Steps 7 & 8. Does the "public generally" exception or the .,Iegally required
participation" exception apply?
Even if a material financial effect on a public official's economic interest is
reasonably foreseeable, he or she still may not be disqualified if the financial effect of the
governmental decision on the public official's economic interest is indistinguishable from
the effect on the public generally (section 87ll3;regulation 18700(b)(7); rJgulation
18707(a)) or the official is legally required to participate (section giig3ire;alation


File No. I-06-189
Page No. 6
18708') You have not present ed any facts indicating that either of these exceptions is
applicable in your situation.
If you have other questions on this matter,prease contact me at (916) 322_5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
VJ:meh
